pageDescription: Multiply Length × Width × Average Depth × Shape Factor to get your pool volume. A standard 12 ft × 24 ft pool at 5 ft depth holds 10,771 gallons. Use the calculator above for your exact result in seconds.
importantBody: Average Depth = (Shallow + Deep) ÷ 2. Never use only the deep end — that is the most common mistake and overestimates volume.
resultsSubtitle: US gallons, litres, m³, UK gallons, and estimated water weight
emptyHint: Select your pool shape, enter dimensions and depths, then click Calculate volume.
exampleNote: Example: 12 ft × 24 ft × 5 ft avg depth ≈ 10,771 US gallons.
proTip1: Measure inside wall to inside wall at the waterline, not outside the pool shell.
proTip2: For kidney pools, enter the wider and narrower width at the belly of the curve.
proTip3: Round pools: enter full diameter — the calculator converts to radius automatically.
proTip4: Split irregular pools into two rectangles and add both volumes together.
proTip5: Confirm US vs UK gallons on chemical labels (1 US gal = 3.785 L, 1 UK gal = 4.546 L).
errWidth: Enter a valid width or diameter.
errDepth: Enter valid shallow and deep depths.
